blocks.text: Jacob₂ Quotes Zenos’s Allegory of the Olive Tree: Jacob 5
First Decay and Remedy (pruning, digging, and nourishing)
For behold, thus saith the Lord:
Second Remedy (wild branches grafted in, withered branches burned, young branches transplanted elsewhere)
General Success (additional time and care needed for the third transplant)
Third Remedy (cross-grafting between the main tree and the transplants; the worst branches are burned)
A Plan for One Last Effort (servants are called for additional grafting, pruning, digging, and nourishing; the bad branches are gradually removed)
A Prophecy of a Third Decay and the End of the Vineyard
Jacob₂ Interprets Zenos’s Allegory and Bids Farewell
(with words and phrases borrowed from chapter 5 in bold)
